member.name: Mims Davies
member.role: MP for East Grinstead and Uckfield
member.synopsis: Mims Davies is the Conservative MP for East Grinstead and Uckfield , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. She currently undertakes the roles of Shadow Minister (Women), and Shadow Secretary of State for Wales.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Job Creation: East Grinstead and Uckfield
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking with Cabinet colleagues to support the creation of jobs for young people in East Grinstead and Uckfield.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DBT is supporting the creation of well-paid jobs in East Grimsby by supporting businesses to start, scale, export and attract investment, leading to higher-skilled roles. The Greater Lincolnshire Combined County Authority utilising new devolved funding he...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Suicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Wales, what consideration has there been to promote BS 30480 Suicide and the Workplace standard in Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: BS 30480:2025 (Suicide and the Workplace – Intervention, prevention and support for people affected by suicide) is a voluntary British Standard published by the British Standards Institution (BSI) in November 2025. It provides guidance for organisations o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Suicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what consideration has there been to provide BS 30480 Suicide and the Workplace standard available to businesses in the United Kingdom.
writtenQuestions.answerText: BS 30480 Suicide and the Workplace is a voluntary British Standard published by the British Standards Institution (BSI), the UK's National Standards Body. The standard is available as a free download through BSI's website. As with other voluntary British ...

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Sick Leave
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many NHS staff absences occur in the NHS due to menopause for what figures are available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not publish official statistics on sickness absence due specifically to menopause because menopause is not recorded as a primary sickness absence reason within the national Electronic Staff Record (ESR) sickness absence statistics.The ESR...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Menopause and Periods
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what consideration has been undertaking to adopt British Standard BS 30416 on menstruation, menstrual health and menopause in the workplace standard - guide for NHS staff.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of supporting National Health Service staff experiencing menstruation, menstrual health conditions, and menopausal symptoms.Many principles in British Standard 30416 are already reflected in NHS England guidance, l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: LGBT+ People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of support available to LGBT veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to ensuring that all veterans, including LGBT veterans, have easy access to support when and where it is needed. Last year, we launched the £50 million VALOUR programme, which will make it easier for all veterans to access the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to help improve support for ex-service women.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Women are a crucial part of the Armed Forces community, both through the value of their service and their contribution to society as veterans. Engaging and supporting the women who have served in our Armed Forces in East Grinstead and Uckfield and across ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: West Sussex
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps is she taking to increase funding for youth clubs and youth providers in West Sussex.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In December 2025, DCMS launched “Youth Matters: Your National Youth Strategy”. This ten year strategy will ensure every young person across the country has somewhere to go, someone who cares for them and a community they feel part of. It is funded by over...

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will take steps to ensure that essential mobile connectivity is built into developments at the planning stage.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the 2025 10 Year Infrastructure Strategy, the Government committed to taking a ‘Digital First’ approach to new infrastructure projects. We recognise it is important that the future digital needs of the owners and users of infrastructure, such as roads,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Listed Buildings: East Grinstead and Uckfield
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to support the heritage and listed buildings sector in East Grinstead and Uckfield constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains dedicated to safeguarding our historic environment for both current and future generations. We continue to provide substantial public investment to support heritage across the nation, working alongside key partners including the Nat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry and Tourism: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what discussions she has had with Cabinet colleagues on the potential impact of business rates on the hospitality, tourism, and leisure sectors in East Grinstead and Uckfield constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Budget, the VO announced updated property values from the 2026 revaluation. This revaluation is the first since the pandemic, which has led to significant increases in rateable values for some properties. In recognition of the impact of the revalua...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sports: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what discussions she had with the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport on the adequacy of the provision of sport in schools in East Grinstead and Uckfield constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is working collaboratively with the Department for Culture Media and Sport (DCMS) and the Department for Health and Social Care (DHSC) to strengthen PE and school sport. As a result of this work, we are investing over £1 billion in PE and s...

registeredInterests.category: 6. Land and property portfolio with a value over £100,000 and where indicated, the portfolio provides a rental income of over £10,000 a year

items.text: Type of land/property: Land, other (agricultural land/non-working smallholding with light industrial and storage units) Location: Mid-Sussex Ownership details: Part ownership with any income paid to my brother, who manages this as he has done for my parents before their deaths and he continues in day to day charge of any matters relating to this family asset. (Registered 5 June 2015; updated 9 January 2020 and 9 September 2024)

A regular volunteer radio presenter of a retro weekend show for the local community radio station, Mid Sussex Radio (previously Burgess Hill Radio). Date interest arose: 24 October 2021 (Registered 18 November 2021; updated 30 May 2022 and 9 September 2024)
